mechanism_narrative: A persistently low and stable federal funds rate near the zero lower bound keeps the equity discount rate compressed, supporting continued risk asset appreciation. When FEDFUNDS trends flat-to-down over the trailing 3 months, forward S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days are positively biased due to sustained accommodative financing conditions for firms and investors.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.39% as of 2014-05, near historic lows
regime_hint: any regime with near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-trend periods do not outperform baseline mean return at p>0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below its trailing 6-month low, this confirms a durable labor market improvement that raises expected corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving S&P 500 gains over the following quarter. The mechanism fails if the unemployment decline is driven by falling labor force participation rather than job creation.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below its trailing 6-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% as of 2014-05, declining trend since 2010
failure_condition: In more than 40% of trigger events, forward 60d SPX returns are negative, or PAYEMS growth is flat/negative concurrently with UNRATE decline, invalidating the improvement interpretation.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that jointly supports corporate revenue growth across cyclical sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. Neither series alone is sufficient; the interaction of both rising confirms demand breadth versus a single noisy series.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 355655 million and HOUST at 791k units as of 2014 snapshot
regime_hint: any regime with synchronized consumer and housing demand growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint RSAFS-HOUST growth interaction term with forward 20d returns is not statistically distinguishable from the IC of either series alone (delta IC < 0.02), indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: During a mid-cycle expansion, sustained positive industrial production (INDPRO) growth signals durable supply-side capacity utilization gains that translate into earnings growth and multi-quarter equity appreciation. In late-cycle or contraction regimes, the same INDPRO growth signal loses predictive power because capacity gains no longer map to forward earnings surprises.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.1173 as of 2014-05, consistent with mid-cycle expansion
failure_condition: INDPRO growth positively predicts forward 60d returns in expansion regime but the effect reverses sign or Rank IC falls below 0.02 when tested in late-cycle subsamples, or overall IC never exceeds 0.03 in any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) growth is a fast-moving signal of aggregate labor demand; positive month-over-month PAYEMS growth predicts short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns as markets price in stronger near-term consumption and corporate revenue prospects within one trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136167 thousand persons as of 2014-05, rising trend
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly PAYEMS growth and forward 5d SPX return stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or announcement-window returns show no significant asymmetry between positive and negative payroll surprises.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above a 2.5% threshold, markets begin pricing in earlier-than-expected Fed tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. Below this threshold, inflation changes have negligible impact on policy expectations and equity pricing.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 216.71 as of 2014-04, YoY inflation near 2%
regime_hint: rising inflation regime with policy normalization risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 55% of trigger instances despite CPI exceeding threshold, or Rank IC in triggered subsample is not more negative than the unconditional Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive real GDP growth (GDPC1) and accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) jointly confirm that aggregate output expansion is being validated by end-consumer demand, reducing recession risk and supporting sustained S&P 500 gains over the following quarter. If GDP growth is positive but RSAFS decelerates, the divergence signals inventory-driven rather than demand-driven growth and the positive equity signal should not hold.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11712.3 billion as of 2014Q1, RSAFS at 355655 million as of 2014-05
regime_hint: any regime with synchronized output and consumption growth
failure_condition: The joint GDPC1-RSAFS growth interaction fails to outperform a single-series GDPC1 model by Rank IC margin of at least 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime with near-zero policy rates, a declining UNRATE combined with an accommodative FEDFUNDS level reinforces investor confidence that growth can continue without imminent tightening, producing short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns. Outside this regime (i.e., once FEDFUNDS begins rising materially), the same UNRATE decline instead raises tightening expectations and the positive relationship breaks down.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% and FEDFUNDS at 0.39% as of mid-2014, consistent with post-recession recovery
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: The positive UNRATE-FEDFUNDS interaction effect on forward 5d returns does not reverse or weaken to Rank IC < 0.02 once FEDFUNDS rises above 1%, or the effect is statistically indistinguishable from the UNRATE-only signal in the recovery regime.
